title: Robert Ingersoll: The Great Agnostic
speaker: Documentary
description: Documentary exploring the life and legacy of Robert G. Ingersoll, America's most famous agnostic orator.

title: Christopher Hitchens — God Is Not Great
speaker: Christopher Hitchens
description: Hitchens presents the arguments from his book God Is Not Great: How Religion Poisons Everything.

title: Bertrand Russell — Why I Am Not a Christian
speaker: Bertrand Russell
description: Rare interview footage of Bertrand Russell discussing his views on religion, God, and the afterlife.

title: Carl Sagan — Pale Blue Dot
speaker: Carl Sagan
description: Sagan's famous reflection on the Voyager 1 photograph of Earth from 6 billion kilometers away.

title: Daniel Dennett — Breaking the Spell
speaker: Daniel Dennett
description: Philosopher Daniel Dennett argues for studying religion as a natural phenomenon using the tools of science.

title: Sam Harris — The Moral Landscape
speaker: Sam Harris
description: Harris argues that science can determine human values and that morality should be approached empirically.

title: Richard Dawkins — The God Delusion
speaker: Richard Dawkins
description: Dawkins presents the central arguments from The God Delusion at the Randolph-Macon Woman's College.

title: Matt Dillahunty — The Superiority of Secular Morality
speaker: Matt Dillahunty
description: A concise case for why secular morality is superior to religious morality.

title: Julia Sweeney — Letting Go of God
speaker: Julia Sweeney
description: Comedian Julia Sweeney's one-woman show about her journey from Catholicism to atheism.

title: Thomas Paine and the Promise of America
speaker: Harvey Kaye
description: Historian Harvey Kaye discusses Thomas Paine's radical democratic vision and its relevance today.

title: Daniel Dennett — The Magic of Consciousness
speaker: Daniel Dennett
description: Dennett's TED talk on how consciousness is less mysterious than it seems — a materialist account of the mind's greatest trick.

title: Steven Pinker — Is the World Getting Better or Worse?
speaker: Steven Pinker
description: Pinker makes the data-driven case for Enlightenment values — that by almost every measure, human life has improved dramatically.

title: AronRa — Foundational Falsehoods of Creationism
speaker: AronRa
description: A systematic demolition of creationist arguments, presented with encyclopaedic knowledge of paleontology, genetics, and taxonomy.

title: Julia Galef — Why You Think You're Right Even If You're Wrong
speaker: Julia Galef
description: Galef introduces the distinction between soldier mindset (defending beliefs) and scout mindset (seeking truth) — the foundation of her book on rational thinking.
